signals.message: Detected wheel-like joints around Z and used the remaining span contrast to infer forward/up.
Nominal span estimate: X=1.000, Y=0.300, Z=0.650.

evidence.label: Geometry span
evidence.details: Shortest span was treated as the best up candidate: X=1.000, Y=0.300, Z=0.650.

evidence.label: Geometry span
evidence.details: Forward span X=1.000 vs lateral Z=0.650.

evidence.label: Joint axis votes
evidence.details: Revolute/prismatic world-axis votes: X=0.00, Y=0.00, Z=2.00.

evidence.label: PCA forward cue
evidence.details: Principal-axis asymmetry suggests forward +y. Primary basis=1.000 -0.000 0.000.

evidence.label: PCA up cue
evidence.details: Principal-axis shape suggests up +z. Secondary basis=0.000 0.987 -0.163, tertiary basis=0.000 0.163 0.987.

conflicts: PCA up cue suggests +z, while the final basis kept Y as up.

assumptions: Orientation is inferred from the nominal zero-pose URDF or expanded XACRO output.
Signed forward and up directions use geometry asymmetry and can stay ambiguous on highly symmetric robots.

suggestedRotate90.note: This only aligns the guessed up-axis to the target up-axis. Use apply-orientation when forward sign matters too.
